reviews.title: Poor Durability – Lining Ripped During Second Run
reviews.reviewText:
I own another pair of Lululemon lined shorts that I really liked, I ran a marathon in them a few years ago, and I continue to wear them to this day. So with another marathon coming up, I decided to get a new pair of the Pace Breaker Lined Short 7".

Unfortunately, after just two runs, the lining ripped, leaving a large hole in the crotch area. The first run was just an easy run to test them out, but during the marathon, the tear caused a lot of chafing, making the rest of the run uncomfortable and the recovery worse.

For shorts marketed for “running and training,” I expected better durability. These didn’t hold up like my previous pair, and I wouldn’t recommend them for long-distance running.

But I just bought the lined version and a seam runs down the middle and causes some very uncomfortable rubbing of the sensitive areas. Pretty dissapointed with this change on liner design since all of my other older lined pace breakers are SUPER comfy

reviews.title: Some steps forward some steps back
reviews.reviewText:
I have several pairs of the previous generation of lined pace breakers and I will say the new liner is much more comfy and fitted than before. There’s also a nice pouch so the drawstring laces don’t rub against your skin. 

The waist on these is a decent amount wider than before, so I would advise sizing down if you’re concerned about that.

And lastly the fit as others have noted is disappointingly more relaxed than before.

reviews.title: Decent upgrade
reviews.reviewText:
Needed to update my old PBs, wear them to train and run. 

Liner is a big upgrade way more comfortable.

Other than that it’s an “alright short” I think lulu missed by making the short larger/baggier than before and it seems they fit a touch bigger than before.

reviews.title: Pace Breaker Lined Shorts “Updated”
reviews.reviewText: Just another example of fixing something that’s not broken. The old shorts were my favorite. These seem like a knockoff. The liner and shorts material is extremely thin, and almost feels cheaply made. The size now feels big and the elastic band straps do practically nothing. Have fun tying 10 knots. Disappointing to say the least.

reviews.title: A good alternative
reviews.reviewText: I went to the store looking for longer shorts in black color, found these instead and felt comfortable with them. They work great for my yoga practice.

reviews.title: Good, could be better with some small improvements
reviews.reviewText: Good shorts overall - I wish there were 3 improvements. 1) The drawstring is on the inside of the shorts, not exactly the most comfortable when they are bunched up - would be just as easy to put them on the outside, 2) the tag is massively long and goes right down your butt - seems like it should be relocated - they are lined shorts so its not like I'm wearing underwear with them, 3) I wish they had a small loop at the bottom to hang them on a hook after a workout so the liner could naturally hang out and dry better to minimize smells - its a small ask and right now I just use a clip to hang them up. Used just for running and they do what I expect - minimize chaffing, but all lined shorts kinda do that.

reviews.title: Getting ready for summer yoga with new shorts
reviews.reviewText: The lined short after a short time works well.  I did not respond with a review right away because my right leg got stiff and I got back to normal after doing some tree poses, some cosmic dancer poses natarajasana and some alternative leg stretches janusirasana.

reviews.title: Amazing shorts, while they last
reviews.reviewText: I have had 9 pairs of these shorts, because they are in fact the perfect running short. Comfortable, designed not to chafe if you have thighs that actually touch, ample pockets, phone pocket on the liner, and all that. The reason I only give 4 stars and have had 9 pairs is that after about a year and a half, the liner comes apart at the same seam, every.single.time. The seam in question is the one in the 'taint' area, which means instant chaffing once it starts to separate. I have gone through 5 pairs in the past few years with the exact same problem. Unfortunately, submitting requests to customer service led to "we cant do anything, wear and tear". Anyways, great shorts but they don't last as long as my Rabbit or Nike.

photos.caption: the weak spot in the seam of a particularly sensitive area for men

reviews.title: Great quality shorts, one issue
reviews.reviewText: Overall, love the shorts. Very comfy, good material, washes well, no chafing while running. My issue is the phone pocket on the left leg. If you have a larger style phone (iPhone Pro Max), the spandex pocket will NOT fix your phone especially with a case. I got mine in there after working it and it just felt like it was gonna tear. I just put the phone in the zipper pocket on the right and it doesn’t jangle around too much but takes some getting used to.

reviews.title: Perfect Marathon shorts
reviews.reviewText:
Great running shorts - No chafing!  I recently ran the Phoenix Marathon and was very pleased with the performance of these shorts!  Particularly the liner which offered superb protection from thigh chafing.  No tears in the shower after this run!  The outer layer did not bunch up at all.  The drawstring was thick and easy to tie and untie for bathroom breaks!  Overall I’m highly satisfied!  I’m not a stick figure guy, so I’ve always fought with running shorts that bunched up or where the liner was too loose and chafed anyway.  
As far as sizing, I’m 5’10” and 180#.  In other brands, I usually select a large running short.  With these, because I was looking for a tight fit on the inner liner, I went with a medium and I got exactly what I wanted.  Do a little research, plug in your measurements to ChatGPT and let it help you select the size based on what you’re looking for.

**Pace Breaker Lined Shorts Review**

The Pace Breaker Lined Shorts from [Brand Name] are a solid choice for anyone looking for comfortable and versatile athletic shorts. The built-in liner provides excellent support and prevents chafing, making them ideal for running, training, or just lounging around. 

**Pros:**

* **Comfortable liner:** The liner is soft and supportive, eliminating the need for underwear during workouts.
* **Lightweight and breathable:** The fabric is lightweight and breathable, keeping you cool and comfortable even during intense activity.
* **Good range of motion:** The shorts offer a good range of motion, allowing you to move freely during any activity.
* **Multiple pockets:** The shorts have multiple pockets, including a zippered back pocket for secure storage.
* **Durable:** The shorts are well-made and durable, able to withstand regular wear and tear.

**Cons:**

* **Price:** The Pace Breaker Lined Shorts are on the pricier side, but the quality justifies the cost.
* **Liner fit:** Some users may find the liner to be too tight or too loose, depending on their body type.

**Overall:**

The Pace Breaker Lined Shorts are a high-quality pair of athletic shorts that are perfect for a variety of activities. The comfortable liner, lightweight fabric, and good range of motion make them a great choice for anyone looking for a versatile and comfortable pair of shorts. 

**Rating:** 4.5 out of 5 stars

reviews.title: A Skeptic turned Believer
reviews.reviewText:
The old version was perfect, so I was wary of an update. But... don't knock it till you try it.

This is even better. Yes, it's roomier, but not by much. Extra 1/2" all around? Still looks sleek. 

The liner feels softer, yet tighter and rides up less. Plenty of room for my substantial manhood, and with phone pockets on BOTH sides.

The cinching string is more durable. Hopefully it'll survive my dryer unlike the old one. 

Overall, a nice update! Happy to transition to this going forward.
